_comment: The ANNOTATED business-process + expected-authz oracle. flow_map.py emits the observed SKELETON (flows / anon access matrix / candidate invariants); the recon/mapper agent annotates the app's REAL intended behavior here. The logic + access-control lenses test each documented invariant + expected-authz cell; the verifier judges a diff a VIOLATION only against this intent (pitfalls.md: accepted-value != bug unless it breaks a rule stated here). Delete this example and write the real map to business_process_map.json; skip it entirely for a thin API / static site (state that as a scoping choice).

flows.name: checkout
invariants: price is server-authoritative — the client sends item id + qty, never the price
a coupon applies once per order and is not stackable
step 3 (/pay) requires steps 1-2 completed server-side (no skip-to-pay)

flows.name: registration
invariants: an account is inactive until the emailed verification token is used (no login pre-verify)
registering an already-registered email must NOT take over / expose the existing account
